summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Standardized residual'?

"Standardized residual" means: (O - E)/√E for each cell; values beyond ±2 indicate that cell contributes notably to the overall χ² statistic. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Cell contribution to χ²'?

"Cell contribution to χ²" means: (O - E)²/E for each cell; identifies which observed-expected discrepancies drive the overall test result. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Practical vs. statistical significance'?

"Practical vs. statistical significance" means: A significant χ² test says the association exists, but effect size measures (Cramér's V, phi) quantify how strong it is. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Cramér's V'?

"Cramér's V" means: V = √(χ²/(n·min(r-1, c-1))); ranges from 0 to 1, measuring the strength of association between two categorical variables. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Limitations of chi-square'?

"Limitations of chi-square" means: Chi-square tests require expected counts ≥ 5 in all cells; small expected counts inflate χ² and may invalidate the test. The other options describe different concepts.
